# Research - 2026-06-20

Focus: **all** (CLI, providers, tools, MCP, commands, hooks, skills, plugins, agents, permissions)

> 1-day delta on 2026-06-19. Yesterday's #1 (`sanitizeOpenAISchema`)
> and #2 (default `properties: {}`) shipped: now in
> `src/providers/transform.ts:131-208` and `src/mcp/client.ts:148,160`.
> Yesterday's #3 (MCP `onprogress`) is open issue #804 still NOT
> labeled `auto-implement`. Three NEW items today, all from cline CLI
> v3.0.28/29: #11475 default-on truncation, #11598 parallel hint in
> tool descriptions, kilocode #11475 subagent permission inheritance.
> aider/claude-code changelog-only. opencode #32815 (Copilot headers)
> and #33040 (TUI error inline) N/A for our bins.

## Executive Summary

- **cline #11475 -> Item #1 (~6h)**: `truncate.ts` is opt-in (8
  tools). MCP results, `edit`, `write`, `apply-patch`, `multiedit`
  return raw payloads. One 200KB MCP response poisons the session.
  cline pivoted from a 7-tool allowlist to default-on at the
  message-builder boundary.
- **cline #11598 -> Item #2 (~2h)**: `bash.ts:76`, `edit.ts:37`,
  `multiedit.ts`, `read.ts`, `grep.ts` descriptions don't tell the
  model to emit independent calls in the same response. System
  prompts say it; descriptions render every turn even with prefix
  cache.
- **kilocode #11475 -> Item #3 (~3h)**: `subagent-permissions.ts`
  already implements denials-only inheritance, but `task.ts:230-242`
  has the integration commented as TODO. Subagents inherit nothing
  from parent rules. Pure wiring fix.
- **opencode #32815 -> N/A**: Copilot plugin headers; no Copilot
  provider here.
- **opencode #33040 / kilocode #11464 -> N/A**: TUI/webview
  rendering issues for surfaces we don't have.
- **cline #11478 -> deferred**: 200k assistant text cap; our agentic
  loop emits structured tool calls, not embedded XML markup. Revisit
  if we ship a Cline-style XML dialect.
- **Carryover**: #804 unlabeled, same blocker as 06-19.

## Detailed Findings

#### 1. Default-on tool-result truncation at message boundary (Category: core, ~6h)

- **What**: New `src/core/messageBuilder.ts` step run before
  `getProviderForModel(...)` receives messages. Two limits:
  `ALEXI_MAX_TOOL_RESULT_CHARS` (default 8_000, every tool result),
  `ALEXI_MAX_FILE_CONTENT_CHARS` (default 50_000, top-level user
  attachments only). Add `ALEXI_MAX_TOTAL_TEXT_BYTES` (6_000_000)
  budget guard with middle-truncation as last resort (rewriting
  bytes mid-transcript invalidates provider prefix cache). Keep
  existing tool-local `truncateOutput` calls as first-line defense.
  Exempt `read` (its own pagination).
- **Why**: `src/mcp/client.ts:481-485` returns whatever the MCP
  server sends, full size. `edit`/`write`/`multiedit`/`apply-patch`
  have no truncation. cline measured this exact failure: their
  previous opt-in allowlist (7 tools) let MCP results bypass both
  per-result cap AND budget collection, so one huge MCP payload
  made the budget loop uselessly shrink other tools' results.
- **Source**: cline/cline#11475 (commit `f5aa035a`, 2026-06-19,
  `+899 -100`, message-builder.ts).
- **Category**: core. **Size**: medium (~6h).

#### 2. Parallel-tool-call hint in tool descriptions (Category: tool, ~2h)

- **What**: Append "When independent reads, searches, or edits are
  also needed, emit those tool calls in the same response instead
  of splitting across turns." to descriptions of `bash`, `shell`,
  `read`, `grep`, `glob`, `codesearch`, `edit`, `multiedit`. For
  `bash`/`shell` also: "Include multiple commands in the same call
  when they are independent and safe to run concurrently." Keep
  `batch` tool unchanged.
- **Why**: cline already had parallel guidance in system prompts
  (same as our `anthropic.txt:7-8`, `code.txt:79`), but benchmark
  smoke tests showed models still serialized independent reads
  turn-by-turn. Fix is to repeat per tool description because
  descriptions render every turn even when system prompt is
  prefix-cached. ~4 lines per tool, no execution change.
- **Source**: cline/cline#11598 (commit `299a4a95`, 2026-06-19,
  `+8 -6`, definitions.ts + system.ts).
- **Category**: tool. **Size**: small (~2h).

#### 3. Wire deriveSubagentSessionPermission into task tool (Category: tool, ~3h)

- **What**: Plumb `parentSessionPermission: PermissionRule[]`
  through `ToolContext` (`src/tool/index.ts`), populated by
  `streamingOrchestrator.ts` at agent dispatch. Uncomment
  `task.ts:235-240` and call `deriveSubagentSessionPermission`.
  Add regression test mirroring kilocode's `task-nesting.test.ts`:
  parent denies `bash "rm *"`, child allowlist `bash "*"`, child
  must still be denied.
- **Why**: Subagents currently inherit nothing from parent
  `tools`/`disabledTools`. A user in plan mode dispatching via
  `task` gets a fresh permission context where the subagent can
  call `write`/`bash` with no inherited deny. kilocode #11475 fixed
  the inverse (over-inheritance) by narrowing to denials only -
  exactly what `subagent-permissions.ts:23-99` already does. Blocker
  is purely wiring.
- **Source**: kilocode/kilocode#11475 (commit `3d4ccc25`,
  2026-06-19, `+90 -4`, kilocode/tool/task.ts).
- **Category**: tool. **Size**: small (~3h).

## Actionable Recommendations

1. **Ladder by risk**: ship #3 first (~15 LOC + test, wires existing
   helper). Then #2 (prompt-only text). #1 in its own PR with env
   matrix and cline test fixture.
2. **Queue blocker persists**: #804, #775, #776, #768 still at zero
   `auto-implement` labels. Land #773 or run `gh issue edit 804 775
   776 768 --add-label auto-implement`.
3. **Polling cadence per #772**: aider 29-day silence, claude-code
   7-day changelog-only - demote both to weekly to cut `gh api`
   budget ~40% with no observed coverage loss.
